Event: Resists the temptations of the One Ring
Explanation: Protects his soul and agency while greatly aiding others by not falling to corruption.

Event: Uses Andúril, a powerful reforged heirloom, in battle
Explanation: Gains combat advantage and helps allies by wielding a superior weapon against foes.

Event: Uses a Palantír and wrenches it out of Sauron's control
Explanation: Risks himself but gains crucial informational and psychological advantage for his side.

Event: Chooses not to use the One Ring despite understanding it
Explanation: Maximizes his integrity and security while keeping others safer from misuse of the Ring.

Event: Fights back-to-back with Gimli at Helm's Deep
Explanation: Improves his survival odds and contributes heavily to the defense.

Event: Engages multiple Nazgûl alone and drives them off
Explanation: Takes personal risk but successfully protects companions from deadly enemies.

Event: Leads a Batman Gambit to attack the Black Gate as a distraction
Explanation: Risks his life but strategically distracts Sauron to aid Frodo and all free peoples.

Event: Personally challenges and goads Sauron through the Palantír
Explanation: Draws Sauron’s attention onto himself, endangering himself but greatly helping the overall plan.

Event: Shouts battle cries while charging into combat
Explanation: Boosts his own fighting spirit and allies’ morale with no real downside.

Event: Helps the Hobbits with swordwork training
Explanation: Invests time/effort with modest gain to himself while strongly improving their skills and survival chances.

Event: Keeps the Hobbits safe during battles
Explanation: Risks himself but significantly increases the Hobbits’ survival and mission success.

Event: Takes on an entire army of Orcs so Frodo can escape
Explanation: Great personal danger but directly preserves Frodo and the quest.

Event: Runs away from responsibilities as rightful king
Explanation: Harms his own status and delays his destiny while also depriving others of needed leadership.

Event: Avoids becoming king out of fear of becoming a tyrant
Explanation: Short-term self-loss but reduces risk of future harm to many; net protective for others.

Event: Uses both bow and sword depending on battle phase
Explanation: Improves his own combat effectiveness and helps his side’s chances in battle.

Event: Assumes leadership of the Fellowship after Gandalf falls
Explanation: Takes on burdens and risk of command while giving the group needed direction and cohesion.

Event: Accepts symbolic tokens (Evenstar, knife, bracers, Andúril) that strengthen his resolve
Explanation: Gains confidence and power while honoring others’ faith in him, which in turn supports their cause.

Event: Orders Boromir to get the Fellowship moving after Moria
Explanation: Sacrifices short-term popularity/comfort to keep everyone alive by avoiding orcs at nightfall.

Event: Tracks Uruk-hai relentlessly for days to find Merry and Pippin
Explanation: Exerts himself greatly but uses his skills to rescue captured allies.

Event: Lets out a grief-stricken wail upon thinking Merry and Pippin are dead
Explanation: Emotional collapse drains his resolve briefly but has no benefit or harm to others.

Event: Contacts Sauron and declares he does not fear him
Explanation: Provokes Sauron’s focus onto himself, a risk that furthers the distraction strategy for others’ benefit.

Event: Faces and survives the warg-cliff fall incident
Explanation: Endures severe danger yet returns alive to continue leading and fighting.

Event: Overthrows the Enemy before becoming king and marrying Arwen
Explanation: Accepts huge risk and effort for his own fulfilled destiny and massive liberation of others.

Event: Undertakes his kingship quest largely because of love for Arwen
Explanation: Pursues personal happiness through assuming a role that greatly benefits many.

Event: Releases the army of the dead after Pelennor Fields as he promised
Explanation: Gives up a powerful asset to keep his word, bolstering moral authority and justice for the dead.

Event: Refuses to follow his destiny for many years
Explanation: Delays his own fulfillment and leaves his people without a rightful ruler for a long time.

Event: Resists offers to take the Ring from Frodo
Explanation: Protects himself from corruption and ensures Frodo can continue his vital mission.

Event: Chooses Frodo’s mission “For Frodo” when Sauron tempts him
Explanation: Rejects personal temptation to fully commit himself and the army to supporting Frodo’s success.

Event: Maintains a long interspecies friendship with Legolas and Gandalf
Explanation: Builds strong alliances that aid him and create effective, cooperative resistance.

Event: Stops Théoden from killing Wormtongue to prevent more bloodshed
Explanation: Intends mercy but unintentionally preserves an enemy agent, enabling severe later losses at Helm’s Deep.

Event: Chooses exile instead of leading his people
Explanation: Abandons power and influence for solitude, weakening his own position and denying leadership to his realm.

Event: Defuses tensions between Legolas and Gimli when they get too fight-happy
Explanation: Spends effort mediating but prevents internal conflict and keeps the team functional.

Event: Leads the defense of the Hornburg at Helm’s Deep
Explanation: Heavily risks himself while greatly improving the fortress’s survival chances.

Event: Leads the army of the dead at Pelennor Fields
Explanation: Gains overwhelming force that secures victory for his side at high personal spiritual and political risk.

Event: Leads the united Men of the West at the Black Gate
Explanation: Risks his life as front-line commander while providing indispensable strategic leadership.

Event: Scarily tracks the Uruk-hai and deduces Merry and Pippin are alive
Explanation: Uses tracking skills to instill hope and guide an effective rescue.

Event: Decapitates the Mouth of Sauron to stop his taunting
Explanation: Eliminates a demoralizing enemy speaker, boosting his side’s morale with minimal strategic downside.

Event: Honors Boromir by wearing his vambraces thereafter
Explanation: Strengthens his own resolve and symbolically upholds a promise that inspires others.

Event: Leads years of wandering as a Ranger, gaining wide knowledge
Explanation: Improves his own competence and later provides rich experience to better serve his people.
